question_text: Which statement best describes what happens when a spring is stretched within its elastic limit?

options.label: A
options.text: The spring will permanently stay stretched and never return to its original shape

options.label: B
options.text: The extension is proportional to the force applied, and the spring returns to its original length when the force is removed

options.label: C
options.text: The spring becomes harder to stretch the more you pull it

options.label: D
options.text: The force needed decreases as the spring stretches

explanation: Within the elastic limit, springs obey Hooke's law: the extension is proportional to the force. When you stop pulling, the spring bounces back to its original shape because the material hasn't been permanently damaged.
